jokes.text: Let me take out Gino's balls in a plastic bag... The balls will never read. They're not gonna read as well. Balls will read. Why won't balls read?
jokes.setup: Director discussing prop logistics for mob scene
jokes.punchline: Larry's earnest defense of severed testicles as readable cinematic props
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David moment - taking absurd film logistics completely seriously. The repetition of 'balls will read' is peak character comedy.

jokes.text: You know, 'Seinfeld,' we did two takes and we were done.
jokes.setup: Director wants multiple takes
jokes.punchline: Larry comparing current situation to his more efficient past show
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry move - invoking his past success to complain about present circumstances.

jokes.text: It's hard to take anything you say seriously right now. You just look crazy.
jokes.setup: Larry in mob movie costume/makeup worrying about his father
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's deadpan observation about his appearance undermining his concern
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy setup - the absurdity of Larry's appearance contrasting with serious family concern.

jokes.text: Is she dead? Er, yeah. Dead, dead, she's dead.
jokes.setup: Father's rambling, evasive explanation about Larry's mother
jokes.punchline: The casual, almost stuttered delivery of devastating news
jokes.explanation: Masterful dark comedy - the repetitive 'dead, dead, she's dead' makes the horrible news almost comical in its bluntness.

jokes.text: Let's see, the funeral was on Monday... so she had to die on...
jokes.setup: Larry asks when his mother died
jokes.punchline: Father works backward from funeral date like solving a math problem
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor in treating death as a logistical scheduling issue

jokes.text: The last words she said to me, 'If anything happens to me, you don't bother Larry. Don't spoil his trip in New York.'
jokes.setup: Larry's outrage at missing the funeral
jokes.punchline: The absurd selflessness that caused the problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect Curb logic - the mother's considerate last words create the very situation that would horrify her.

jokes.text: Your mother didn't say not to call Andy. She said not to call you, that's all she ever said.
jokes.setup: Larry realizing his cousin Andy was called and attended
jokes.punchline: The specific exclusion of only Larry makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - making Larry's exclusion seem deliberate and personal rather than general consideration.

jokes.text: Abe Lincoln, he's Jewish. He got 'shot in the temple.'
jokes.setup: Reference to Uncle Harold's famous joke at funeral
jokes.punchline: The terrible pun about Lincoln being shot in the temple/Jewish temple
jokes.explanation: Classic dad joke/groan-worthy pun. Works as character detail but not sophisticated humor.

jokes.text: That bat mitzvah, oh, God. Is there anything worse? Can you think of anything worse?
jokes.setup: Being reminded about Julie Blum's bat mitzvah
jokes.punchline: Larry's dramatic overreaction to a normal social obligation
jokes.explanation: Pure Larry - turning minor social events into existential crises. Good character moment.

jokes.text: Larry's mother just passed away. And we're going to take some time with that.
jokes.setup: Larry wanting to cancel dinner plans
jokes.punchline: Using his mother's death as excuse immediately after learning about it
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cringe comedy - Larry immediately exploiting his tragedy for social convenience, delivered through Cheryl's diplomatic phrasing.

jokes.text: My mom died. Yeah. So I'm just not gonna be doing much for a while.
jokes.setup: Phone call with Michael about lunch
jokes.punchline: Larry's matter-of-fact deployment of his mother's death as social shield
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the social excuse pattern, showing Larry getting comfortable with using his tragedy.

jokes.text: Well... my mother died. Tell Julie good luck, sorry I can't be there. And mazeltov.
jokes.setup: Calling to cancel bat mitzvah attendance
jokes.punchline: The incongruous 'mazeltov' after using death as excuse
jokes.explanation: Nice character touch - Larry maintaining Jewish social niceties even while using death as excuse.

jokes.text: He saw me at the Improv screaming at the audience one night in New York... he thought I could play this tough Jew
jokes.setup: Friend asks how Larry got the movie role
jokes.punchline: Scorsese cast him based on seeing him genuinely angry at a comedy club
jokes.explanation: Great meta-humor about Larry's real personality landing him acting roles - 'tough Jew' is perfectly specific

jokes.text: Lonely, lonely... Lonely... Just keep repeating it.
jokes.setup: Larry asking for a meditation mantra
jokes.punchline: Jeff giving him 'lonely' as a mantra, the opposite of meditative peace
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor - the anti-mantra that would make meditation worse, not better.

jokes.text: Who's gonna stop us? Nobody. It's totally applicable.
jokes.setup: Larry asking if you can share mantras
jokes.punchline: Jeff's confident dismissal of mantra protocol rules
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the absurd mantra logic - treating spiritual practice like sharing property.

jokes.text: I kind of need to be alone with my own thoughts. Oh, absolutely. Jeez, bless your heart.
jokes.setup: Ed wanting to do 'stop-and-chat' about his daughter
jokes.punchline: Larry using mother's death to escape social interaction, Ed's immediate sympathetic response
jokes.explanation: Another iteration of the death-as-social-escape pattern, showing how effectively it works.

jokes.text: My mother had a tattoo on her ass? On her right buttock, yes, sir.
jokes.setup: Learning about tattoo disqualifying burial
jokes.punchline: The specific anatomical detail making it more absurd
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - each detail makes the situation more absurd. Larry's incredulous repetition is perfect.

jokes.text: She saw a tattoo parlor. She said, 'Hey, let's do it.' Your parents had tattoos. With our names.
jokes.setup: Explanation of how Larry's proper Jewish mother got tattoo
jokes.punchline: The romantic spontaneity completely at odds with her character
jokes.explanation: Great character revelation - the prim mother having a wild romantic moment that now causes religious problems.

jokes.text: I can't imagine my parents making love. I can't imagine you making love
jokes.setup: Father reminisces about romantic tattoos
jokes.punchline: Larry's expanding horror at parental sexuality
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from general discomfort to personal attack on his father's sexuality

jokes.text: There's no good Chinese food in LA. A country of one billion people couldn't send one good chef to Los Angeles.
jokes.setup: Defending his choice to not order Chinese food
jokes.punchline: The absurd scale of his complaint about Chinese food availability
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry escalation - turning minor food preference into sweeping demographic impossibility. The population statistic makes it absurdist perfection.

jokes.text: A country of one billion people couldn't send one good chef to Los Angeles
jokes.setup: Larry's Chinese food theory
jokes.punchline: Taking the logic to ridiculous demographic extreme
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Larry's ability to take minor complaints to absurd logical extremes

jokes.text: You can't be an East Indian-giver
jokes.setup: Lewis wants the mantra back
jokes.punchline: Racially specific variation on 'Indian giver'
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay but relies on outdated terminology - typical Larry cultural insensitivity

jokes.text: Splitting it? What is this, a timeshare in the Hamptons? You don't split a mantra.
jokes.setup: Richard Lewis wanting to share the mantra
jokes.punchline: Larry comparing spiritual practice to real estate investment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant analogy joke - reducing mystical practice to crass materialism. The Hamptons reference is perfectly Larry.

jokes.text: It strikes me as being a tad... homosexual.
jokes.setup: Discussing sharing a mantra
jokes.punchline: Larry's completely illogical homophobic reasoning
jokes.explanation: Dated humor relying on homophobic assumptions. Shows Larry's character flaws but not sophisticated comedy.

jokes.text: You know, it was just that I've been going through a rough time, what with my mother and all. I didn't even have a chance to say good-bye.
jokes.setup: Cheryl rejecting Larry's sexual advances
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately pivoting to emotional manipulation using his mother's death
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cringe moment - Larry weaponizing genuine grief for sexual purposes. Shows how low he'll sink.

jokes.text: I only got four shovels. Asshole.
jokes.setup: Larry's group arriving to dig up the grave
jokes.punchline: Gravedigger calling Larry an asshole for not bringing enough shovels
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - even the corrupt gravedigger finds Larry annoying. Shows universal irritation with Larry.

jokes.text: Ji ya, ji ya, ji ya, I made it out of clay. And when it's dry and ready, oh, ji ya I will play
jokes.setup: Larry meditating with the mantra while digging
jokes.punchline: The mantra accidentally forming the dreidel song
jokes.explanation: Masterful callback that creates accidental wordplay. The mantra morphing into the dreidel song is perfect Jewish comedy synchronicity.

jokes.text: You know what it means? 'Fuck me.' Fuck me! Fuck you!
jokes.setup: Richard discovering the mantra's meaning
jokes.punchline: The innocent meditation word being profane, leading to mutual cursing
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to the mantra storyline. The innocent meditation becoming accidental profanity is perfect comedy irony.

jokes.text: Sometimes I like to pretend that I'm deaf and I try to imagine what it would be like... not to be able to hear them. It's not so bad.
jokes.setup: Peaceful moment discussing bird sounds
jokes.punchline: Larry's characteristically dark take on disability
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry darkness - taking a beautiful moment and finding the morbid angle. Perfect character consistency.

jokes.text: I was in this movie, and they gave me this counterfeit money... My mother died...
jokes.setup: Being arrested for counterfeit bills
jokes.punchline: Larry's two-part defense: movie explanation followed by mother's death
jokes.explanation: Final deployment of the mother's death excuse, showing Larry still trying to use it even when arrested.

jokes.text: Ji ya!
jokes.setup: Being arrested for counterfeit money from the movie
jokes.punchline: Larry's meditation mantra as his final exclamation under arrest
jokes.explanation: Perfect episode ending callback. The mantra that caused so much trouble becomes Larry's final word in defeat.
